person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Patrick Murray of Ochtertyre MP
Gender: Male
Birth: 1422
Falahill,Eddleston,Peebles-Shire,Scotland
Death: 1476 (53-54)
Philiphaugh, Scottish Borders, Scotland, United Kingdom
Immediate Family:
Husband of Katherine Balfour
Father of John Murray of Blackbarony; Elizabeth Murray; David Murray, 2nd of Ochtertyre; William Murray; Patrick Murray; Isabella Murray; William Murray; John Murray, of Falahill, "the Outlaw" and Sir John Murray of Stanhope, Kt.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre於1422年出生在蘇格蘭皮布萊爾艾德萊斯頓時，他的父親Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight32歲，母親Margaret Colquhoun51歲。他和Katherine Balfour至少有1個兒子。他去世於1476年在英國蘇格蘭斯可迪夏 波德斯享年54歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre于1422年出生在苏格兰皮布萊爾艾德萊斯頓时，他的父亲Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight32岁，母亲Margaret Colquhoun51岁。他和Katherine Balfour至少有1个儿子。他去世于1476年在英国苏格兰斯可迪夏 波德斯享年54岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Murray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Moray in northeastern Scotland, which derives from Celtic mori- ‘sea’ + treb- ‘settlement’. The founder of the Scottish house of Murray was a Fleming named Freskin who was granted Strathbrock in West Lothian and Duffus in Moray by David I. The family took its name from the region in the late 12th century.
Irish and Scottish: shortened form of   McMurray  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muireadhaigh ‘descendant of Muireadhach’ a personal name meaning ‘mariner’. Occasionally it may be a shortened form of McMurray .

variants.name: Murri
meanings: Probably Scottish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Murry
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Murrah
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Murray  .

variants.name: Murie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel.
Scottish: habitational name from Murie in Errol (Perthshire).
Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Merhi
meanings: Arabic (mainly Lebanon and Syria): from a variant of the personal name Mirhi of unknown meaning. Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians. Compare Murray 5.

variants.name: Murrey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: McMurray
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muireadhaigh or Mac Muireadhaich, a patronymic from the personal name Muireadhach ‘seafarer’ (see Murdoch ). Compare McCurry 1.

variants.name: Murra
meanings: Italian (Sicily): topographic name from Sicilian murra ‘large rock, mass of rocks’ or a habitational name from a place called with this word.
East Frisian: unexplained.
Albanian: nickname from murrë (definite form murra) ‘dark gray cow’.

variants.name: Murrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Murray  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Patrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Name of the apostle and patron saint of Ireland ( c.389–461 ), Gaelic Pádraig. He was a Christian Briton and a Roman citizen, who as a young man was captured and enslaved by raiders from Ireland. He escaped and went to Gaul before returning home to Britain. In about 419 he felt a call to do missionary work in Ireland. He studied for twelve years at Auxerre, and in 432 returned to Ireland. For the rest of his life it is difficult to distinguish fact from fiction. He apparently went to the court of the high kings at Tara and made some converts there; then he travelled around Ireland making further converts until about 445 , when he established his archiepiscopal see at Armagh. By the time of his death almost the whole of Ireland is said to have been converted to Christianity. He is also credited with codifying the laws of Ireland. In his Latin autobiography, as well as in later tradition, his name appears as Patricius ‘patrician’ (i.e. belonging to the Roman senatorial or noble class), but this may actually represent a Latinized form of some lost Celtic (British) name. In Ireland in particular, it has been one of the most enduringly popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre was born in 1422, in Eddleston, Peeblesshire, Scotland, his father, Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight, was 32 and his mother, Margaret Colquhoun, was 51. He had at least 1 son with Katherine Balfour. He died in 1476, in Scottish Borders, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 54, and was buried in Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre est né en 1422, à Eddleston, Valle de Twede, Écosse, son père, Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Margaret Colquhoun, avait 51 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Katherine Balfour. Il est décédé en 1476, à Scottish Borders, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre 1422 in Eddleston, Peeblesshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight 32 und seine Mutter Margaret Colquhoun 51. Er hatte mit Katherine Balfour mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1476 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Scottish Borders, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre è nato il nel 1422 nel Eddleston, Peeblesshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Colquhoun, aveva 51 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Katherine Balfour. Morì nel 1476, nel Scottish Borders, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyreが1422年にスコットランドペエブレシュアイアエッドレストンで生まれた時，父Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knightは32歳，母Margaret Colquhounは51歳であった。彼はKatherine Balfourとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1476年にイギリススコットランドスコッティッシュ ボルダースで54歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre(이)가 1422년 스컷랜드 피블레실르 에들레스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Colquhoun(은)는 51세였다. 그는 Katherine Balfour(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1476년 영국 스코틀랜드 스콧이시 볼더스에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre nasceu em 1422, em Eddleston, Peeblesshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Colquhoun, tinha 51 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Katherine Balfour. Ele faleceu em 1476, em Scottish Borders, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre родился в 1422 г. в местности эддлестон, пиблессхир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Colquhoun, 51. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Katherine Balfour. Он умер в 1476 г. в местности скоттиш бордирс, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Patrick Murray of Dollerie and Ochtertyre nació en 1422, en Eddleston, Peeblesshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir David Murray - Lord of Tullibardine, Knight, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Margaret Colquhoun, tenía 51 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Katherine Balfour. Murió en 1476, en Scottish Borders, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Escocia, Reino Unido.
